contexts.description: Manages deployment targets and configurations for agents — currently supporting local CLI execution and Claude Managed Agents cloud target. Includes deploy/redeploy flows, per-run environment setup, and tier-gated target availability.
contexts.name: agent-deployment

contexts.description: Multi-tab agent configuration editor covering system prompt, model selection, tool bindings, connector assignments, and parameters. Users compose an agent's identity and capabilities here before deployment. Reads persona state from the Zustand agent store and persists changes via Tauri IPC.
contexts.name: agent-editor

contexts.description: Visual identity system for agents — supports uploading custom icons, AI-generating icons via Leonardo/Higgsfield, and resolving the correct icon for display throughout the app. The frontend glyph tab wraps Rust icon-generation commands and stores assets locally.
contexts.name: agent-glyph

contexts.description: Per-agent health dashboard showing uptime, error rates, last-run outcomes, and healing event history. Surfaces health signals from the observability backend and lets users trigger manual health checks. Sits inside the agent editor as a dedicated tab.
contexts.name: agent-health-ui

contexts.description: Test suite framework for validating agent behavior against defined use-cases and output assertions. Supports single-run tests, parameterized suites, and a Lab mode for comparing multiple model configurations side-by-side. Results feed into the health score pipeline.
contexts.name: agent-testing

contexts.description: Manages discrete use-case scenarios attached to a persona — each defining expected inputs, outputs, and success criteria. Used both as documentation and as the basis for automated test suite generation. Backed by a Rust command module and linked to the test runner.
contexts.name: agent-use-cases

contexts.description: Automated error recovery system that detects execution failures, analyzes root causes via LLM, generates fix strategies, and optionally retries. Tracks healing attempts on a timeline and escalates to incidents when healing fails repeatedly. Integrates with the alert system for notifications.
contexts.name: ai-healing

contexts.description: Metrics aggregation and visualization layer providing usage analytics, execution statistics, cost breakdowns, and leaderboard rankings across personas. Uses Recharts for charting and connects to both the event store and execution history to derive insights.
contexts.name: analytics-dashboard

contexts.description: Image and media generation plugin integrating Leonardo AI and Higgsfield for visual asset creation. Supports background removal, Gemini-powered image analysis, SVG generation, and video/audio processing via ffmpeg and transcription. Generated assets are stored locally and linked to personas or knowledge entries.
contexts.name: artist-generation

contexts.description: Interactive LLM-driven design sessions where a user converses with an AI to build or refine an agent template step-by-step. The engine parses AI responses into structured build actions, applies quality gates, and supports fix-pass loops for failing validations. Distinct from runtime execution — this is design-time.
contexts.name: build-sessions

contexts.description: Cloud backup, workspace synchronization, and remote command infrastructure. Supports encrypted workspace snapshots, conflict-merge resolution, Supabase-backed cloud storage, and remote approval workflows. Enables cross-device persona access and out-of-app management.
contexts.name: cloud-sync

contexts.description: AI companion assistant (Athena) embedded in the app — supports conversational interaction, proactive suggestions, decision support, project tracking, fleet bridge integration, browser testing, and voice input. Maintains a long-term brain memory and can be triggered by observability events.
contexts.name: companion-ai

contexts.description: OAuth2 flows, connector readiness evaluation, auto-discovery of available desktop credentials, and desktop bridge integrations (Keychain/Windows Credential Store). Supports CLI credential capture, credential foraging, and schema-proposal-based inference of required credentials from a persona's tool usage.
contexts.name: connector-auth

contexts.description: AES-256-GCM encrypted credential vault for storing API keys, tokens, and secrets. Provides CRUD for credentials, catalog browsing, dependency tracking, and database connection management. The Rust layer handles encryption/decryption; the frontend vault section exposes a catalog-based discovery UI.
contexts.name: credential-vault

contexts.description: SQLite database schema, migration pipeline, and repository pattern for all persistent data. Uses R2D2 connection pooling and Rusqlite. All schema changes go through versioned migrations; the repository modules expose typed query functions consumed by command handlers.
contexts.name: database-layer

contexts.description: Shared component library of 173+ reusable UI primitives organized by category (buttons, forms, modals, display, feedback, layout). Maintains a machine-generated CATALOG.md. Components enforce design tokens (typo-*, rounded-*, shadow-elevation-*) and semantic color via ESLint rules.
contexts.name: design-system

contexts.description: Developer productivity plugin offering codebase scanning (KPI scan, standards scan, idea scanner, context generation), incremental analysis, and an HTTP debug server. Surfaces structured scan results and lets developers drill into context-mapped findings. Powers the Vibeman context-map integration.
contexts.name: dev-tools-plugin

contexts.description: AI meta-persona (the Director) that periodically reviews all persona executions, issues DIRECTOR_VERDICT assessments, writes long-term strategic notes to its Brain memory, and surfaces a business-value rollup. Acts as an autonomous quality overseer distinct from individual persona runs.
contexts.name: director-orchestration

contexts.description: Central event bus that collects, stores, and distributes typed events generated by persona executions, triggers, and system processes. The frontend overview events tab visualizes these in a filterable timeline. Includes shared event relay for cross-persona event propagation.
contexts.name: event-tracking

contexts.description: Core Rust runtime that spawns and manages agent execution processes — setting up credentials, environment variables, team context, and dispatching to the LLM provider via structured prompt stages. Returns a stream of typed events consumed by the frontend execution inspector.
contexts.name: execution-runner

contexts.description: Cron-based and on-demand scheduling of agent executions. The Rust scheduler evaluates cron expressions, manages next-run times, and fires executions into the runner queue. The frontend surfaces scheduled runs in the Cron Agents overview and allows users to define per-persona schedules.
contexts.name: execution-scheduling

contexts.description: Frontend execution inspector showing real-time logs, subagent fan-out trees, event streams, and final outputs for each persona run. Subscribes to the Tauri event bridge for live updates during an active execution. Includes a mini-player for compact background-run monitoring.
contexts.name: execution-ui

contexts.description: Process and terminal management plugin — provides a pseudo-terminal (PTY) for running CLI sessions, transcribes sessions for replay, discovers running processes, manages git hooks sidecars, and integrates with the skills pipeline. The Fleet view surfaces running CLI agents as tiles.
contexts.name: fleet-terminal

contexts.description: GitLab SCM integration — provides repository listing, merge request management, pipeline status, and issue tracking directly in Personas. Backed by a dedicated Rust GitLab module and frontend store slice. Allows personas to be triggered by or act on GitLab events.
contexts.name: gitlab-integration

contexts.description: System-wide health dashboard aggregating per-agent health scores, uptime stats, and SLA compliance metrics. The Rust healthcheck module runs periodic probes and writes results consumed by the overview health page and the per-agent health tab.
contexts.name: health-monitoring

contexts.description: 14-language internationalization system supporting per-section lazy-loaded locale bundles. The en.json source file is split into section chunks at build time via split-locales.mjs, generating TypeScript types for compile-time key safety. The t proxy loads sections on demand; route-based preloading prevents first-paint flashes.
contexts.name: i18n-system

contexts.description: Tracks execution failures and policy violations as incidents with severity classification, continuation logic, and promotion to formal audits. Incidents can be auto-created by the healing system when recovery fails, and manually reviewed through the overview incidents UI.
contexts.name: incident-management

contexts.description: Abstraction layer over LLM providers (currently Claude/Anthropic). Manages API calls, streaming responses, model selection, cost calculation, and token budget enforcement. Supports Bring-Your-Own-Model (BYOM) configuration for alternative endpoints.
contexts.name: llm-provider

contexts.description: Queue for executions and decisions that require human approval before proceeding. Surfaces ambiguous outputs, policy-blocked actions, and remote approval requests from the Companion. Integrated with the cloud approval workflow for out-of-app review via remote commands.
contexts.name: manual-review-queue

contexts.description: Model Context Protocol (MCP) gateway layer — manages MCP tool credentials, exposes a local MCP server (mcp_bin) for external AI clients to invoke Personas capabilities, and integrates MCP gateway credentials into the execution engine. Includes a dedicated MCP server binary.
contexts.name: mcp-gateway

contexts.description: Obsidian vault synchronization plugin — mirrors Obsidian markdown notes into the app's knowledge graph, runs semantic and syntactic lint passes, resolves conflicts, and provides graph analysis for traversal and insight extraction. Used by personas for long-term memory and context injection.
contexts.name: obsidian-brain

contexts.description: Peer-to-peer LAN networking layer using QUIC transport, mDNS discovery, and Ed25519 identity. Enables LAN enclaves for local multi-device persona sharing, device identity management, manifest synchronization, and bundle transfer. Gated behind the 'p2p' Cargo feature.
contexts.name: p2p-network

contexts.description: Core CRUD and lifecycle management for AI personas (agents). Handles creation, configuration, duplication, archival, and listing of persona profiles stored in SQLite. The Rust command layer exposes IPC commands consumed by the React personas page and sidebar navigation.
contexts.name: persona-management

contexts.description: Reusable automation recipe library — pre-packaged workflows that can be adopted, versioned, and adapted to a persona's context. Includes matching logic to suggest relevant recipes, eligibility checking, AI-powered generation, and an interactive playground for testing.
contexts.name: recipe-system

contexts.description: Research utilities plugin providing structured web research, source aggregation, and citation management. Backed by a Rust research_lab command module and a dedicated store slice. Designed to feed findings into the knowledge base and companion brain.
contexts.name: research-lab

contexts.description: Application-wide settings management — appearance, notifications, API keys, network, engine capabilities, data portability, and tier limits. Settings are persisted in SQLite via Rust command handlers and cached in a Zustand system store slice.
contexts.name: settings-system

contexts.description: Zustand-based global state with 60+ domain slices organized under agents/, overview/, pipeline/, system/, and vault/. Uses useShallow for selective subscriptions and globalThis singletons for HMR-safe buffers. The store root files wire slices together into typed store instances.
contexts.name: state-management

contexts.description: Tauri IPC bridge layer — the tauriInvoke wrapper (enforced over raw invoke by ESLint), auto-generated command names, and the event bridge for reactive event subscriptions. All frontend-to-backend communication flows through this layer with timeout enforcement.
contexts.name: tauri-ipc

contexts.description: Task assignment orchestration — matching tasks to the most capable team persona via heuristics and AI, tracking completion status, and surfacing assignments in the team workspace. The engine handles assignment matching and handoff logic between personas.
contexts.name: team-assignments

contexts.description: Interactive visual canvas and red-room secure decision space for teams — supports whiteboard-style collaboration, goal visualization, and high-stakes decision framing in an isolated environment. Powered by React Flow for graph rendering.
contexts.name: team-canvas

contexts.description: Team-scoped memory and knowledge base — stores synthesized insights, memories, and shared context that all personas on a team can reference. Backed by the team memories Rust module and surfaced in a dedicated workspace view. Supports AI-powered team synthesis.
contexts.name: team-knowledge

contexts.description: KPI definition, binding, derivation, and evaluation system for team performance measurement. KPIs are derived from execution outcomes and goal progress via Rust engine modules. The frontend KPIsPage and DevKpi bindings surface current metrics and allow teams to define targets.
contexts.name: team-kpis

contexts.description: Core team CRUD, channel management, and workspace coordination for multi-persona teams. Teams group personas under shared goals with dedicated communication channels. The Rust backend handles team state and channel delivery; the frontend provides a workspace shell with sub-views.
contexts.name: team-management

contexts.description: Agent template library with preset configurations, team presets, and diagram-based visual representations. Templates can be adopted mid-build via AI-suggestion (Glyph mid-build flow), versioned through a build-session pipeline, and validated with template checksums. Includes n8n-compatible templates.
contexts.name: template-system

contexts.description: Event-driven trigger engine that fires persona executions in response to webhooks, cron schedules, file events, and manual invocations. Includes a Studio UI for visual trigger construction, a dead-letter queue for failed deliveries, a Smee relay for local webhook testing, and cloud webhook relay.
contexts.name: trigger-system

contexts.description: Semantic vector knowledge base using FastEmbed + ONNX Runtime for local embeddings. Stores chunked text with SQLite-Vec, powers semantic search across personas and team knowledge. Gated behind the 'ml' Cargo feature for builds that need ML inference.
contexts.name: vector-knowledge-base

contexts.description: Visual workflow designer and compilation pipeline for composing multi-step agent pipelines with branching, looping, and conditional logic. The compiler transforms a visual workflow graph into an executable artifact. Includes n8n-format import/export and platform rule enforcement.
contexts.name: workflow-design
